'''Kane Linnett''' (born 11 January 1989) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer, who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Queensland Rugby League Northern Division#Mackay & District Rugby League|Sarina Crocodiles]] in the [[Queensland Rugby League Northern Division#Mackay & District Rugby League|Mackay & District Rugby League]].

He has previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] and the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]]. Linnett was a member of the [[North Queensland Cowboys|Cowboys]]' [[2015 NRL Grand Final|2015 NRL Premiership]] and [[2016 World Club Series#World Club Challenge|2016 World Club Challenge]] winning sides.

On 6 November 2023, Linett announced his retirement with immediate effect.

==Background==
Linnett was born in [[Shellharbour, New South Wales]], Australia.

He grew up in [[Barrack Heights, New South Wales|Barrack Heights]], a suburb of [[Shellharbour, New South Wales|Shellharbour]].

He attended [[Warilla High School]] and played junior football for both the Port Kembla Blacks and Windang Pelicans. While at high school, Linnett was a promising [[rugby union]] player, representing [[New South Wales]] Country High Schools, but ultimately decided to stick with [[rugby league]].<ref>http://www..couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/kane-linnett-hoping-to-make-a-centre-position-his-own-with-nrl-club-noth-queensland-cowboys/story-e6frep5x-1226278659030 {{Dead link|date=March 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
[[File:KANE LINNETT.jpg|Linnett playing for the [[Newtown Jets]]|250px|thumb]]

===Early career===
In 2007, Linnett played for the [[Illawarra Steelers]] in the [[SG Ball Cup]], earning a contract with [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.smithyspeaks.com.au/smithy-speaks-with-kane-linnett/|title=Smithy Speaks with Kane Linnett}}</ref>

Linnett played with the [[St George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] side between 2008 and 2009, scoring 31 tries in 51 games, before joining the [[Sydney Roosters]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

[[File:Kane Linnett.jpg|Linnett playing for the [[Sydney Roosters]]|125px|thumb|right]]

==Sydney Roosters (2010–11)==
Linnett made his [[National Rugby League]] début in round 14 of the [[2010 NRL season]]. He ran for over 200m and scored a try in the [[Sydney Roosters]]' 38–6 win over [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].<ref>[http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/kane-more-able-when-going-gets-tough/story-e6frfgbo-1225901862662 Herald Sun] {{dead link|date=March 2023}}</ref>

He finished the season as a member of the [[Sydney Roosters]] [[2010 NRL Grand Final]] team that lost to his former club, [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]], by a score of 32–8.

In 2011, Linnett was limited to just 10 [[NRL]] games. In round 23, he played on through a season-ending knee injury, scoring a try in the [[Sydney Roosters]]' 20–12 victory over the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

On 3 August 2011, he signed a two-year contract with [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]].

==North Queensland Cowboys (2012–18)==
Linnett scored his first try for the [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] in the side's round 3 match against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].

Two weeks later, in the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] round 5 win against the [[Canberra Raiders]], Linnett set a club record for most metres by a [[North Queensland Cowboys]] {{rlp|ce}}, with 234.

He was named the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]' 'Most Improved' for 2012, at the club's annual presentation ball.

In 2013, Linnett played in every single game for the [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] club scoring 9 tries.

At the end of the 2013 season, Linnett represented [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]], qualifying to play for them through his mother.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23552641|title=NRL centre Linnett commits to Scots|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

On 11 February 2014, Linnett re-signed with the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] for a further two seasons.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/linnett-signs-longterm-contract-cowboys-8465/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150525161757/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/linnett-signs-longterm-contract-cowboys-8465/ |archive-date=25 May 2015 |title=Linnett signs long-term contract with Cowboys {{!}} NRL {{!}} Zero Tackle}}</ref>

Later that month, Linnett was a part of the [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] club's victorious [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]] team and was subsequently named in the 'Team of the Tournament.

In round 3 of the [[2015 NRL season]], Linnett played his 100th [[NRL]] game.

On 4 October 2015, he was a member of the [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] [[2015 NRL Grand Final|Grand Final]] winning side, starting a {{rlp|ce}} in the side's dramatic 17–16 victory over the [[Brisbane Broncos]].

On 21 February 2016, Linnett was a member of the [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] [[2016 World Club Series#World Club Challenge|World Club Challenge]] winning side, starting the game at {{rlp|ce}} in the side's 38–4 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley Stadium]].

In May 2016, Linnett represented his [[Country Origin]] for the first time.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.townsvillebulletin.com.au/sport/nrl/cowboys-to-suit-10-players-in-representative-round/news-story/fc0d0baf5ddd8227bd30993544cbbc41|title=Cowboys' perfect 10|newspaper=Townsville Bulletin|date=2 May 2016}}</ref>

In October 2016, Linnett was named in [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]]'s [[2016 Four Nations]] squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.scotlandrl.com/article/14844/grand-final-hero-among-new-look|title = Grand Final Hero Among New-Look Bravehearts}}</ref>

In round 3 of the [[2017 NRL season]], Linnett played his 150th [[NRL]] game in [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] club's 30–8 loss to the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].

In North Queensland's semi-final win over [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]], Linnett played his 150th game for the club.

On 1 October 2017, he started at {{rlp|ce}} in the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]' [[2017 NRL Grand Final]] loss to [[Melbourne Storm|Melbourne]].

Linnett played in all 28 games for the club in 2017, scoring five tries.

Linnett made 12 appearances for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in 2018, as the club struggled towards the bottom of the ladder for the entire season but won their final two games to avoid the wooden spoon.

In September 2018, it was revealed that Linnett was one of several [[North Queensland Cowboys]]' players told that they would not be offered a contract for the 2019 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/cowboys/cowboys-confirm-seven-departures-in-roster-cleanout-including-lachlan-coote-and-kane-linnett/news-story/8da2655ac503b1fe3e9adf8f3ab9794c|title = Cowboys confirm seven departures in roster clean-out, including Lachlan Coote and Kane Linnett|date = 14 September 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/09/13/cowboys-to-farewell-departing-players/|title=Cowboys to farewell departing players|date=13 September 2018}}</ref>

==Hull Kingston Rovers (2019 – 2023)==
On 14 November 2018, Linnett signed a three-year deal with [[Super League]] club, [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.hullkr.co.uk/experienced-nrl-grand-final-winner-kane-linnett-signs/|title = Experienced NRL Grand Final player Kane Linnett signs|date = 15 November 2018}}</ref>

On 9 January 2019, Linnett received the number 23 jersey ahead of the start of the [[Super League]] season.

On 13 January 2019, Linnett made his non-competitive [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' début in a try scoring pre-season friendly against the [[Widnes Vikings]], Linnett claimed a 30–16 victory with his new club.

Linnett made his first competitive appearance for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in round 1 of the 2019 [[Super League]] season, Linnett who started the game in the {{rlp|sr}} recorded a thrilling 18-16 victory over cross-city rivals [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].

On 17 February 2019, in round 3 in a game against the [[London Broncos]], Linnett scored his first try in the [[Super League]].

In round 4 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored a [[hat-trick]] in Hull KR's 26-6 victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56817938|title=Hull Kingston Rovers 26-6 Leeds Rhinos - Hosts register second straight win|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In round 8 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Linnett scores two tries for Hull KR in a 40-16 victory over Leigh.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-40-leigh-16-victory-reward-for-returning-robins-fans-3255624/|title=Hull KR 40 Leigh 16 - Victory reward for returning Robins fans|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
Linnett made a total of 20 appearances for Hull KR in the [[2021 Super League season]] including the club's 28-10 semi-final loss against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com|date=30 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|website=www.bbc.com|date=23 September 2021}}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Linnett played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
In round 23 of the 2023 Super League season, Linnett scored two tries for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 52-10 victory over Leigh in the Challenge Cup final rematch.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66592489|title=Super League: Hull KR 52-10 Leigh Leopards - Robins avenge Challenge Cup defeat|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

Linnett played 24 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2023 }}</ref> On 6 November 2023, Linnett announced his retirement from rugby league.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67339724|title=Kane Linnett: Hull KR second rower announces retirement from rugby league|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
